Bv=2.853 kips TMS 402-22 Eq 8-8 (kSba/Ba)5/3+(kSbv/Bv)5/3 = 0.000 â‰¤ 1.00 0.000 PASS â–¶ Calc TMS 402-22 آ§8.1.4.3.3 / Eq 8-8 â€” Combined Tension and Shear Interaction Interaction = ( k S ba Ba ) 5/3 + ( k S bv Bv ) 5/3 â‰¤ 1.0 = ( 0.000 4.969 ) 5/3 + ( 0.000 2.853 ) 5/3 = 0.000 + 0.000 = 0.000 â‰¤ 1.00 âœ“ k = 1.00 (no increase applied) D/C = 0.000 1.00 = 0.000 Engineering Notes: Design per TMS 402-22 ASD آ§8.1.4.3. Applies to headed anchor bolts or bent bar anchors (standard hook) fully embedded in grouted masonry. lbe (edge distance): Per TMS آ§6.3.7, measured from the edge of the masonry to the center of the bolt â€” auto-computed as b/2 for a bolt centered in the wall thickness. Override with the manual field if the bolt is positioned differently. Apt: د€lbآ² per Eq 6-5, less the projected area lying outside the masonry (beyond each wall face) and less overlap with adjacent anchors at spacing S (no portion of masonry counted more than once). Apv: د€lbeآ²/2 per Eq 6-6 (half-cone toward the free edge), with the same overlap adjustment. (TMS آ§6.3.2, آ§6.3.3) Tension (Ba): Lesser of masonry breakout (1.25Aptâˆڑfâ€²m) and steel tensile (0.5AbFu). Fu capped at min(1.9fy, 125 ksi). Shear (Bv): Minimum of four failure modes: masonry breakout (Bv1), pryout/crushing (Bv2 = 580(Abfâ€²m)0.25), masonry shear cone (Bv3), and steel shear (Bv4 = 0.25AbFu). Combined Check (Eq 8-8): (kآ·Sآ·ba/Ba)5/3 + (kآ·Sآ·bv/Bv)5/3 â‰¤ 1.0. The â…“ allowable stress increase, when checked, is applied as k = 3/4 on the demands â€” consistently in the individual checks and the interaction. No â…“ stress increase: k = 1.0. Minimum embedment (TMS آ§6.3.6): Lb,min = greater of 4د• and 2â€³.
